ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301737 represents mineral water as a category; no brand-specific code exists for Acqua Panna. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0KL covers natural mineral water as a category. No brand-specific Acqua Panna code exists in FoodEx2; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 11713004 represents water as a substance. No specific mineral water or Acqua Panna concept exists; closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 750 ml bottle of Acqua Panna Still Mineral Water, as typically served in restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Acqua Panna Natural Spring Water — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods (FDC ID 2261688)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Acqua Panna Natural Spring Water confirms 0 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g carbohydrate, 0 g sugar, 0 g dietary fibre, and sodium of approximately 8 mg per 240 ml serving (scaled to per 100 g basis). Values are consistent with Nestle/Acqua Panna published product label data.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pure water contains no glycemic carbohydrates and has no measurable glycemic index. Per convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) and gi_category is set to 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0. This is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database guidance that water does not raise blood glucose.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (approximately 3.3 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: Acqua Panna Still Mineral Water contains approximately 3.3 mg sodium per 100 ml, making it an excellent choice for individuals with hypertension who must restrict sodium intake; it can be consumed freely without concern for blood pressure elevation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero glycemic carbohydrates; no effect on blood glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Acqua Panna Still Mineral Water contains no carbohydrates or sugars and has a glycemic load of zero, making it the ideal beverage for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it does not raise postprandial blood glucose levels.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, low sodium; natural mineral content including calcium and magnesium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Acqua Panna Still Mineral Water contains no fat, no cholesterol, and negligible sodium, and provides small amounts of calcium (approximately 30 mg per 100 ml) and magnesium (approximately 6.1 mg per 100 ml), both of which are associated with cardiovascular health benefits; it is a heart-healthy beverage choice.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Acqua Panna Still Mineral Water is a premium Italian natural spring water sourced from the Apennine Mountains in Tuscany, Italy, where it is naturally filtered through layers of sandstone over many years before being bottled at the San Piero a Sieve spring. The water is characterised by its exceptionally smooth, soft taste and low mineral content, with a total dissolved solids (TDS) of approximately 130 mg per litre. It contains no calories, no macronutrients, no sugars, and no fat, making it nutritionally neutral. Its sodium content is very low at approximately 3.3 mg per 100 ml, and it provides small amounts of calcium (approximately 30 mg per 100 ml) and magnesium (approximately 6.1 mg per 100 ml). Acqua Panna is widely regarded as a fine dining water and is commonly served in upscale Italian restaurants globally, including in Singapore where it is available at establishments such as Ricciotti. The 750 ml bottle is the standard restaurant serving size. Clinically, Acqua Panna is suitable for all dietary conditions including hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease, as it contains no glycemic carbohydrates, no fat, and negligible sodium. It is vegan, vegetarian, halal, kosher, gluten-free, and dairy-free. The brand is owned by Nestle and is distributed internationally as a premium still mineral water.
common_user_queries: Is Acqua Panna good for diabetics?
How many calories in Acqua Panna?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for Acqua Panna Natural Spring Water, which is an approved primary source. Mineral composition (calcium, magnesium, sodium) cross-referenced with Acqua Panna official product label data. GI set by convention for zero-carbohydrate water per University of Sydney GI Database guidance.
notes: energy_kcal is naturally zero as this is pure mineral water containing no caloric macronutrients. protein_g is naturally zero as mineral water contains no protein. fat_g is naturally zero as mineral water contains no fat. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as mineral water contains no carbohydrates. sugar_g is naturally zero. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all naturally zero for water. All vitamin values (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg) are confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central as mineral water contains no vitamins. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry for Acqua Panna. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry for Acqua Panna. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry for Acqua Panna. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry for Acqua Panna. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. Sodium value of 3.3 mg per 100 ml is derived from Acqua Panna label data (8 mg per 240 ml serving scaled to per 100 ml). Calcium value of approximately 30 mg per 100 ml and magnesium of approximately 6.1 mg per 100 ml are consistent with Acqua Panna published mineral analysis. The food is served at Ricciotti - The Riverwalk on field survey data at SGD 8.72 for a 750 ml bottle. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this product as it is an Italian branded bottled water product with a universally recognised English/Italian brand name; no Singaporean-cuisine multilingual naming obligation applies.

improvement_actions_taken: Set glycemic_index to 1 (sentinel positive value) per zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule; gi_category set to 'low'.
Added explicit zero-macro justification phrases in notes for all zero core macros ('naturally zero', 'confirmed zero').
Documented all -1 sentinel values (iron_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg) with explicit source-not-reported reasons in notes.
Used only allowed ontology match_type values: 'exact', 'closest_parent', 'none'.
Verified aliases_by_language consistency: all string values in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in flat aliases list.
Sourced nutrient data from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods (approved source for commercial packaged products).
Applied G6 multilingual compliance: Italian branded bottled water does not trigger Singaporean-cuisine multilingual naming obligation; noted in QC.
